qa.user_verbatim_complaint: 现在所有 specialist agents（ui-specialist、architect、product-owner、user）给出的报告在 dev 工作流中与 BA/dev/QA 的 context 传递不连贯，且缺乏 handoff contract。具体表现：1. UI specialist 报告使用自然语言描述视觉问题（如"按钮间距不一致"），但不包含 CSS selector、measured vs expected 值、源代码位置等 BA/dev 可操作的字段。2. 所有 specialist 与 BA 之间没有 handoff contract，BA 收到 specialist findings 后没有规范化的消费协议（哪些字段必须提取、哪些 claim 需要自行验证、遇到模糊描述如何处理）。3. 结果：BA 和 dev 只能盲人摸象，无法根据 specialist 报告做出确定性的实现决策。目标：修改相关 agent spec 文件，在所有 specialist 输出和 BA 消费侧同时建立 handoff contract。

out_of_scope_observations.observation: AC1 spec text says finding_deterministic has 'additional required fields (axe_impact/computed_value/expected_value)' but the actual schema keeps these as optional properties (no required list in finding_deterministic's allOf branch). The implementation is CORRECT — making them required would be a schema-breaking change. This is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift: the AC text over-states what was implemented. Recommend BA update AC1 to say 'retains optional properties axe_impact, computed_value, expected_value' not 'additional required fields'.

spec_section_updates.section_4: Cycle 20260517-153856 current state: All 6 target files (skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json, agents/ui-specialist.md, agents/architect.md, agents/product-owner.md, agents/user.md, agents/ba.md) were modified. report-schema.json finding_base.required now includes location (structured object), measured, expected, downstream_agent. agents/ba.md line 1127 has Specialist Findings Intake Protocol section with all 5 required sub-elements. However, commit 07d6e32 ('orphan: unattributed session changes') bundles the 6 cycle target files with commands/dev.md and 50 other files. The cycle's specific changes cannot be cleanly distinguished from pre-existing dirty state in the commit history. git diff HEAD~1 HEAD shows commands/dev.md changed (1 line: Chinese→English text substitution in BA goal paragraph).
spec_section_updates.section_6: AC6 is violated at the commit-evidence level: the AC requires commands/dev.md NOT be changed by this cycle (git diff --quiet exits 0), but the orphan commit 07d6e32 that contains the 6 cycle target files also modifies commands/dev.md. While the dev report claims this was pre-existing dirty state, this claim is not independently verifiable from commit history — in /close debate the commit-scoped evidence makes AC6 appear violated. The 'git diff --quiet HEAD -- commands/dev.md' check NOW passes (tree is clean) only because everything was already committed into the same orphan commit.
spec_section_updates.section_7: To close AC6: Either (1) split the orphan commit into two commits — one containing only the 6 cycle target files for task 20260517-153856, and a second containing the pre-existing dirty state including commands/dev.md; or (2) provide a pre-cycle dirty-tree snapshot (git stash list, .last-cleanup timestamp, or a QA artifact timestamped before the cycle started) that proves commands/dev.md was already modified before this cycle began. Option 1 is cleaner for /close audit. Option 2 is lower-risk if git history is considered immutable. The user-requirement-facing implementation (handoff contract fields in all 6 files) is correct and should be preserved regardless of which option is chosen.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: All 6 files were modified: report-schema.json gained structured location/measured/expected/downstream_agent; ba.md gained Specialist Findings Intake Protocol. The handoff contract is established at both the machine-enforced layer (JSON schema) and prose-convention layer.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: Not applicable — this is an agent spec file change, not a UI element verification

qa.overall_assessment: The user-facing implementation (handoff contract fields in all 6 specialist/BA files) is substantively correct and satisfies the verbatim user requirement. All 5 AC content criteria (AC1 through AC5) pass on the substance of what was delivered. However, AC6 fails at the commit-evidence level: the orphan commit 07d6e32 bundles the cycle's 6 target files together with a modification to commands/dev.md, making AC6's 'commands/dev.md not changed' requirement unverifiable from commit history. Codex confirmed this as a close-blocking risk. The spec text for AC1 also has a drift: it says finding_deterministic has 'additional required fields' when the schema correctly keeps them optional — this is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift observation, not an implementation defect.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The root cause was: (1) report-schema.json finding_base.location was a flat string with no selector/file/line decomposition and no measured/expected/downstream_agent fields; (2) architect/product-owner/user prose schemas lacked BA-actionable fields; (3) BA had no intake protocol. All three root causes are addressed: (1) finding_base now has structured location and all required fields; (2) all three agent prose schemas updated; (3) Specialist Findings Intake Protocol added to ba.md.

all_findings.issue: AC6 violated at commit-evidence level: commands/dev.md was changed in the same orphan commit as the 6 cycle target files. The AC spec requires 'git diff --quiet HEAD -- commands/dev.md exits 0 (no changes to out-of-scope files)'. While the working tree is now clean (all committed), the specific violation is that the cycle's changes are bundled in the same commit as a modification to commands/dev.md (1-line Chinese→English text substitution). In /close debate this commit-scoped evidence makes AC6 appear violated — the 'pre-existing dirty state' claim is not independently provable from commit history.
all_findings.recommendation: Option 1 (preferred): Split the orphan commit 07d6e32 into two separate commits — one containing only the 6 cycle target files (skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json, agents/ui-specialist.md, agents/architect.md, agents/product-owner.md, agents/user.md, agents/ba.md) and a separate commit for pre-existing dirty files (commands/dev.md and others). Option 2: Provide a pre-cycle snapshot artifact (timestamped before 2026-05-17T16:30:00Z) that proves commands/dev.md was already modified before this cycle began.

all_findings.issue: spec_text_vs_execution_drift: AC1 says finding_deterministic has 'additional required fields (axe_impact/computed_value/expected_value for deterministic)'. The actual schema at skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json:252-258 has these as optional properties only (no required list in finding_deterministic's allOf branch). The implementation is CORRECT — making them required was not part of this cycle and would be a schema-breaking change. The AC text over-states what was delivered.
all_findings.recommendation: BA should update AC1 wording from 'additional required fields (axe_impact/computed_value/expected_value for deterministic)' to 'retains optional properties axe_impact, computed_value, expected_value in finding_deterministic'. This is a spec text fix only — no implementation change needed.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 2 close-blocking risks: (1) AC6 attribution is not sound from commit evidence — commands/dev.md was changed in commit 07d6e32 alongside the 6 target files; dev report's pre-existing dirty state claim is not independently provable from commit history; proposed fix is to split the orphan commit or provide a pre-cycle snapshot. (2) AC1 has spec/schema mismatch: ticket line 121 says finding_deterministic has 'additional required fields axe_impact/computed_value/expected_value' but actual schema has them as optional properties only; proposed fix is to correct AC1 wording. Codex cleared: AC4 user.md {url, file:null} is sufficient; AC5 tier_3_tainted any-one-missing taint rule is correct; AC1 allOf inheritance is valid.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1 (AC6 commit attribution) incorporated as a critical finding blocks_release:true — the pre-existing claim is not independently provable and /close debate would see this as an AC6 violation. Finding 2 (AC1 spec text drift) incorporated as a minor observation and spec_text_vs_execution_drift — not a blocks_release finding since the implementation is correct and the spec text is the only drift. AC4/AC5/allOf findings cleared.

failed_criteria: AC6: commands/dev.md was changed in the same orphan commit as the 6 cycle target files, making the 'commands/dev.md not changed by this cycle' assertion unverifiable from commit history
critical_issues: Orphan commit 07d6e32 bundles cycle target files with commands/dev.md modification. The dev report's 'pre-existing dirty state' claim cannot be independently verified from the commit. /close debate will flag AC6 as violated based on commit-scoped evidence.
refined_context.recommended_approach: Option 1 (preferred): Use git rebase or cherry-pick to create a clean commit containing only the 6 cycle files. The target commit should include exactly: skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json, agents/ui-specialist.md, agents/architect.md, agents/product-owner.md, agents/user.md, agents/ba.md — and nothing else. Option 2: Provide a dated artifact (timestamp before 2026-05-17T16:30:00Z) showing commands/dev.md was already dirty before the cycle. Note: The actual content of all 6 cycle files is correct and satisfies all AC1-AC5 requirements — no content changes are needed, only commit attribution cleanup.
refined_context.additional_context: The spec_text_vs_execution_drift in AC1 (finding_deterministic 'additional required fields' vs actual optional properties) should be fixed in the BA spec text to prevent future confusion, but does not block this cycle's close since the implementation is correct.
